+{"id":"bd-1rz0.2","title":"Alien Artifact: Formal Resize Scheduler Model","description":"Design a mathematically principled resize scheduler (control‑theory + change‑point detection + e‑processes for anytime validity).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:22.885261170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:02.641824922Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:22.885261170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:24.807096454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien Artifact Math Notes (MANDATORY)

Purpose
- Provide a mathematically principled resize scheduler with anytime validity, explicit loss tradeoffs, and provable bounds on worst‑case behavior.

Core Model
- Treat resize events as a non‑stationary time series with regime shifts.
- Objective: minimize expected loss L(action, state) with asymmetric costs.

Loss Matrix (example)
- keep_reflow | skip_frame
  - smooth (true)  : 0   |  30
  - storm (burst)  : 10  |  1
- Intent: during bursts, skipping a frame is cheap; during steady state, skipping is costly.

Decision Rule (Bayes)
- Maintain posterior over regimes:
  P(storm|data_t) ∝ P(storm) × Π P(x_i|storm)
- Action a* = argmin_a Σ_c L(a,c) × P(c|data_t)

Anytime Validity (e‑process)
- Define e‑value e_t such that E[e_t] ≤ 1 under null (no regime shift).
- Trigger “storm mode” when e_t ≥ 1/α (optional stopping safe).
- This lets the scheduler switch modes without invalidating guarantees.

Output Guarantees
- Bound expected latency under steady state.
- Bound average render cost under storms by throttling to max_fps.
- Explicitly log when bounds are violated.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:02Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","title":"Resize Scheduler: Change-Point Detection (BOCPD)","description":"Design BOCPD-based regime detection for resize streams; output burst/steady regimes with confidence.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.343398831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:11.464618617Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:14.524512097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.343398831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BOCPD Details (Change‑Point Detection)

Model
- Observations: inter‑resize intervals Δt and magnitude |Δsize|.
- Assume piecewise‑stationary hazard λ (probability of regime change).
- Run length r_t tracks time since last change.

Recurrence
- p(r_t, x_t) = Σ_{r_{t-1}} p(r_{t-1}, x_{t-1}) × p(r_t|r_{t-1}) × p(x_t|r_{t-1})
- p(r_t=0|r_{t-1}) = λ (change)
- p(r_t=r_{t-1}+1|r_{t-1}) = 1-λ (no change)

Outputs
- Posterior P(r_t=0|x_1:t) gives change‑point probability.
- Use threshold τ_cp to enter storm mode.

Parameters
- λ chosen from empirical resize patterns (configurable, safe default).
- Likelihood modeled with exponential/gamma on Δt, mixture for |Δsize|.

Guarantees
- Sensitivity to burst onset; bounded false positives via λ and τ_cp.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:11Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1rz0.2.2","title":"Resize Scheduler: Control-Theoretic Frame Pacing (PID)","description":"Define PID/PI controller for frame pacing during resize storms; formal stability notes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.526637079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:20.238452786Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:14.707269019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.526637079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":208,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Control‑Theoretic Frame Pacing (PID/PI)

Goal
- Maintain target frame latency T (e.g., 16–33ms) under resize storms.

Signals
- error e_t = T - render_latency_t
- output u_t = throttle level (max FPS / skip ratio)

PID (or PI) Update
- u_t = Kp*e_t + Ki*Σ e + Kd*(e_t - e_{t-1})
- Clamp u_t to safe bounds [u_min, u_max]

Stability Notes
- Choose Kp, Ki from Ziegler‑Nichols‑style sweep on representative resize storms.
- Prefer PI (Kd=0) to avoid jitter from noisy latency measurements.

Fallback
- If measurements missing, default to conservative throttle.

Guarantee
- Bounded overshoot and monotone convergence to target latency (empirical).
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:20Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","title":"Resize Scheduler: Anytime-Valid Decision Rule (e-process)","description":"Define e-process thresholds for acting under uncertainty without invalidating correctness.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.705030592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:29.575022263Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:14.888461745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.705030592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":209,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Anytime‑Valid Decision Rule (e‑process)

E‑value Definition
- Construct e_t such that E[e_t] ≤ 1 under null (no regime shift).
- Example: likelihood ratio between storm vs steady models.

Decision Rule
- Trigger storm mode if e_t ≥ 1/α (α = tolerated false‑positive rate).
- Safe under optional stopping (resize stream can end anytime).

Computation
- e_t = Π (p(x_t|storm) / p(x_t|steady)) with clipping to avoid underflow.
- Use log‑space accumulation for numerical stability.

Guarantee
- False alarm probability ≤ α regardless of stopping time.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:29Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","title":"Invariant Proof Sketches + Failure Mode Ledger","description":"Proof sketches for invariants, plus explicit failure mode and mitigation ledger.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.888199761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:36.725315440Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:15.070056538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.888199761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":210,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Invariant Proof Sketches + Failure Mode Ledger

Core Invariants
1. Continuous reflow: UI reflects latest size within SLA bound.
2. Atomic present: each frame is internally consistent (no partial clears).
3. No placeholder: real UI always rendered during resize.
4. No ghosting: shrinking clears stale regions deterministically.

Proof Sketches (outline)
- Atomic present: single buffered write + sync output ⇒ no interleaving.
- No ghosting: full clear of removed regions + reset scroll region ensures old cells removed.
- Continuous reflow: coalescer emits latest size each tick; scheduler bound ensures max delay.

Failure Mode Ledger
- Mux without sync output: fallback to overlay redraw + full clear.
- Extremely rapid resize: throttle via PI controller but never stalls input.
- Terminal caps mis-detection: conservative fallback and diagnostic log.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:36Z"}]}
